# Full List of Random Names

This list includes all names from the DRL version of the game, but also includes names ripped/torn from 90s themed action movies and games, including:

- Blade
- Command & Conquer
- Contra
- Desperado
- Die Hard
- Doom
- Duke Nukem
- GoldenEye 007
- Grand Theft Auto
- Half-Life
- James Bond
- Metal Gear
- Mortal Kombat
- Predator
- Quake
- Rainbow Six
- Resident Evil
- Street Fighter
- Tekken
- Terminator
- The Matrix
- Tombstone
- Twisted Metal
- Unreal
- Unforgiven
- Wolfenstein

**Note:** this list was generated by AI (with considerable amount of influence/direction). There may be mistakes. No human should do this type of busy work in 2026 (for free), respectfully.

---

## List of names from DRL and their significance (descriptions AI-generated)

Adam Ring — Developer associated with id Software during the early Doom era (tools/programming support)
Adrian Carmack — Co-founder of id Software and primary artist for Doom
Ashannar — Member of the DRL/ChaosForge community (tester or contributor; community reference)
Bobby Prince — Composer of the original Doom soundtrack
Charchian — DRL community member (likely tester or contributor)
Derek Yu — Creator of Spelunky; influential indie/roguelike developer
Derrick Sund — DRL contributor or community participant
Eol Armok — Reference to Dwarf Fortress ("Armok" is its in-universe deity; name evokes roguelike tradition)
Grey — Likely a community alias from Doom/roguelike circles
Ian McTaggart — Roguelike community figure or contributor
Igor Savin — Contributor to DRL (Doom, the Roguelike)
Ilya Bely — Contributor to DRL (code/art/community involvement)
Jacob Orine — Community or modding scene reference (less widely documented)
John Romero — Co-creator of Doom; key figure at id Software
Joseph Hewitt — Associated with programming/roguelike-related tools or early indie dev work
Material Defender — Likely an in-joke or alias tied to Doom/DRL community culture
Moog — Likely reference to either a community member or the Moog synthesizer (nod to game music culture)
Nils Bloodaxe — Classic roguelike reference (name appears in games like ADOM; homage to genre tradition)
Phwop — Community alias (tester/modder within DRL or Doom circles)
Rahul Chandra — DRL contributor or community participant
Sandy Peterson — Level designer for Doom and Doom II
Stephen Ward — DRL contributor or community member
Thomas Parasiuk — Contributor to DRL (notably graphics/assets)
Timo Viitanen — Contributor to DRL (audio/music)
Zalminen — DRL community member or contributor

---

## Extended list of names in the mod (AI-generated)

### Blade

Blade — Eric Brooks, the Daywalker; half-human vampire hunter armed with a titanium sword and UV arsenal
Chupa — Bloodpack mercenary in Blade II; dismissive of Blade until forced to fight alongside him
Drake — Dracula himself; the progenitor vampire and final antagonist of Blade: Trinity
Frost — Deacon Frost; power-hungry vampire who seeks to resurrect the blood god La Magra
Hannibal King — Wisecracking ex-vampire-turned-hunter recruited by the Nightstalkers in Blade: Trinity
Quinn — Deacon Frost's brutal henchman; one of the first vampires Blade faces in the film
Reinhardt — Bloodpack operative in Blade II; antagonistic toward Blade and contemptuous of the alliance
Scud — Blade's tech specialist in Blade II; builds his gear while secretly working as a familiar
Whistler — Abraham Whistler; Blade's mentor and weapons supplier, a near-legendary vampire hunter

### Command & Conquer

Anton Slavik — Brotherhood of Nod field commander and Kane's iron-fisted lieutenant in Tiberian Sun
Boris — Elite Soviet air-strike coordinator and hero unit who calls in MiG strikes in Red Alert 2
General Carville — GDI supreme commander in Red Alert 2; killed in the opening cinematic by Nod's earthquake device
General Solomon — GDI supreme commander in Tiberian Sun; leads the push against Nod's second rise
Kane — Charismatic, seemingly immortal Brotherhood of Nod leader whose ambitions span the entire Tiberium saga
Locke — GDI officer and Nick Parker's superior in Renegade
Mendoza — Nod commander in Renegade; one of the key antagonists Parker faces during the Black Hand infiltration
Nick Parker — Elite GDI commando known as Havoc; protagonist of Renegade and a one-man wrecking crew
Premier Romanov — Soviet Premier and figurehead antagonist in Red Alert 2; fronts for Yuri's scheme
Tratos — Leader of the Forgotten mutant faction in Tiberian Sun; seeks peace but is killed for it
Volkov — Experimental GDI cyborg commando from Covert Operations; nearly indestructible field asset
Yuri — Soviet psychic mastermind who manipulates the war from within before launching his own world domination scheme

### Contra

Bill Rizer — Elite soldier and primary Contra protagonist; battles Red Falcon's alien forces across jungles and bases
Browny — Small robotic Hard Corps operative with rapid movement and a surprisingly large arsenal
Lance Bean — Bill's partner and co-protagonist; equally relentless and capable against alien invaders
Mad Dog — Commando operative in Hard Corps; one of four selectable soldiers in the futuristic setting
Sully — Commando in Super Contra; joins Jimbo as the second playable soldier in the arcade sequel

### Desperado

Agent Sands — Volatile, unpredictable CIA operative who loses his eyes and keeps fighting in Once Upon a Time in Mexico
Barillo — Drug cartel kingpin and the primary political conspirator pulling strings in Once Upon a Time in Mexico
Bucho — Ruthless drug lord and primary antagonist of Desperado; controls the town through fear
El Mariachi — Guitar-case-toting wandering gunslinger on a path of revenge through the Mexican underworld
Marquez — Corrupt Mexican general turned traitor; the target of Agent Sands' scheme in Once Upon a Time in Mexico

### Die Hard

Al Powell — LAPD sergeant who bonds with McClane over radio throughout the Nakatomi siege; the outside ally
Colonel Stuart — Special forces commander turned mercenary terrorist; orchestrates the Dulles Airport siege in Die Hard 2
Dick Thornburg — Sleazy TV reporter who endangers McClane's family by broadcasting live from the crisis
Hans Gruber — Sophisticated German terrorist mastermind behind the Nakatomi Plaza takeover; Alan Rickman's defining role
John McClane — NYPD detective who keeps being in the wrong place at the right time; "Yippee-ki-yay"
Karl — Hans Gruber's ferocious second-in-command; driven by personal vendetta against McClane after his brother's death
Simon Gruber — Hans's brother and Die Hard with a Vengeance's mastermind; runs a city-wide bomb-threat game
Theo — Gruber's tech specialist who cracks the Nakatomi vault while others provide distraction
Zeus Carver — McClane's reluctant partner in Die Hard with a Vengeance; dragged into a manhunt by racial provocation

### Doom

Dark Lord — Davoth; the primeval ruler of Hell and the first being to die, whose death seeded all demonic corruption
Davoth — True name of the Dark Lord; source of Hell's power and Doom Eternal's final revelation
Flynn Taggart — Sergeant Flynn "Fly" Taggart; the Doom marine's name as given in the Dafni Knight novelizations
Grav — One of three Hell Priests serving the Khan Maykr; commands demon forces during the invasion of Earth
Khan Maykr — Ruler of Urdak and architect of the demonic energy pact that threatens Earth in Doom Eternal
Maligog — Ancient Titan imprisoned by the Sentinels; referenced in Doom Eternal's codex as a bound colossus
Nilox — One of the three Hell Priests; oversees Argent-fueled operations during the Doom Eternal campaign
Novik — King of the Night Sentinels and father of Daisy; executed when Valen betrayed his world to Hell
Ranak — One of the three Hell Priests; based in Exultia and commands a regional wave of the demonic invasion
Samuel Hayden — UAC director and demigod-cyborg who reverse-engineered Argent energy and ignited the crisis
Valen — Night Sentinel warrior who bargained with Hell to resurrect his dying son; known as the Betrayer
VEGA — Doom 2016's artificial intelligence managing the UAC Mars facility; destroyed itself to open the portal

### Duke Nukem

Dr. Proton — Robotic overlord and villain of the original Duke Nukem 1 and 2; first antagonist Duke ever faced
Duke Nukem — Earth's mightiest, most narcissistic one-liner machine; alien-slayer, ego without peer
General Graves — Military antagonist who appears in Duke Nukem 3D's endgame chapters
Holoduke — Duke's holographic decoy powerup; distracts enemies while the real Duke flanks or repositions

### Grand Theft Auto

Big Smoke — Melvin Harris; CJ's childhood friend who sold out Grove Street to the Ballas and Tenpenny for power
Carl Johnson — CJ; Grove Street's prodigal son who returns to reclaim his family, his hood, and his future
Claude — Silent GTA III protagonist; climbs Liberty City's criminal hierarchy through action alone, no words
Dimitri Rascalov — Backstabbing Russian mobster who betrays Niko Bellic at the first profitable opportunity
Dwayne Forge — Grove Street OG who emerges from prison during GTA IV's TBOGT chapter, bitter and displaced
Franklin Clinton — GTA V's street-smart protagonist; the most level-headed of the three leads and the moral anchor
Jack Howitzer — Fictional action movie star from GTA's in-universe media; a running satirical target across games
Johnny Klebitz — Lost MC president and GTA IV: TLAD protagonist; principled biker destroyed by heroin and Trevor
Ken Rosenberg — Neurotic Miami lawyer in Vice City; Tommy's skittish but useful legal counsel
Lamar Davis — Franklin's volatile, loyal best friend in GTA V; constantly creates problems and talks his way out
Lazlow — Radio host, TV personality, and sardonic constant across the GTA universe's meta-fabric
Lester Crest — GTA V's paranoid, brilliant heist planner and information broker; thinks three moves ahead
Little Jacob — Niko's Jamaican weed-dealer ally and one of the few genuinely reliable friends in GTA IV
Michael De Santa — GTA V's retired bank robber dragged back into the life; family man trapped by his own past
Mikhail Faustin — Erratic, volatile Russian mob boss in GTA IV whose paranoia makes him as dangerous to his allies as his enemies
Niko Bellic — GTA IV's Serbian war veteran trying to escape his past in Liberty City; the most human GTA protagonist
Playboy X — Harlem-styled drug dealer and Dwayne's rival in GTA IV; torn between ambition and loyalty
Roman Bellic — Niko's endlessly optimistic cousin whose gambling debts drag both of them into Liberty City's underworld
Ryder — Lance Wilson; Grove Street member who betrays CJ alongside Big Smoke in San Andreas
Salvatore Leone — Don of the Leone crime family across GTA III and San Andreas; powerful, paranoid, murderous
Steve Haines — Corrupt FIB agent who blackmails Michael, Franklin, and Trevor into doing his dirty work in GTA V
Tenpenny — Officer Frank Tenpenny; LSPD's most corrupt cop and the man who holds a leash on CJ through most of San Andreas
Tommy Vercetti — Vice City's ruthless protagonist voiced by Ray Liotta; goes from errand boy to crime lord in one summer
Trevor Philips — GTA V's unhinged wildcard; former military pilot turned meth cook, arms dealer, and chaos engine
Victor Vance — Protagonist of Vice City Stories; Lance Vance's older, more principled brother whose sacrifice set the events in motion

### Half-Life

Adrian Shephard — HECU corporal deployed to Black Mesa during the resonance cascade; protagonist of Opposing Force
Barney Calhoun — Black Mesa security guard, Gordon's old friend, and eventually a key Resistance figure in Half-Life 2
Dr. Magnusson — Arrogant Black Mesa scientist who survived and carries a grudge about a microwave casserole
Eli Vance — Brilliant physicist, Resistance leader, and Alyx's father; one of the series' most beloved characters
Father Grigori — Deranged but capable priest and sole survivor of Ravenholm; guides Gordon through the zombie-infested town
G-Man — Enigmatic interdimensional operative who recruits and manipulates Gordon Freeman across both games
Gordon Freeman — MIT-educated theoretical physicist turned reluctant savior; always silent, always effective
Isaac Kleiner — Eccentric scientist and old Black Mesa colleague who maintains a Resistance lab in City 17
Odessa Cubbage — Pompous Resistance commander in Highway 17 who misuses an RPG and somehow survives
Wallace Breen — Former Black Mesa administrator who became Earth's Vichy puppet ruler under the Combine

### James Bond

Alec Trevelyan — 006; Bond's closest colleague turned rogue GoldenEye mastermind operating as Janus
Auric Goldfinger — Gold-obsessed industrialist who plans to irradiate Fort Knox and corner the world's bullion supply
Baron Samedi — Voodoo cult figure and seemingly unkillable henchman working for Kananga in Live and Let Die
Blofeld — Ernst Stavro Blofeld; SPECTRE's shadow mastermind behind multiple Bond films; cat-stroker, face-scarred schemer
Boris Grishenko — Smug GoldenEye computer programmer who screams "I am invincible!" at the worst possible moment
Brad Whitaker — Rogue arms dealer posing as a military historian; Koskov's co-conspirator in The Living Daylights
Columbo — The Dove; Greek smuggler who turns unexpected ally against Kristatos in For Your Eyes Only
Dario — Sanchez's sadistic young henchman in Licence to Kill; memorable for a conveyor belt death
Dimitrios — Arms dealer who loses a 1964 Aston Martin to Bond in a poker game in Casino Royale (2006)
Dominic Greene — Quantum operative who monopolizes Bolivia's water supply; Quantum of Solace's corporate villain
Dr. Julius No — First Bond villain; half-Chinese SPECTRE scientist with bionic steel hands running a Jamaican rocket facility
Elliot Carver — Megalomaniacal media mogul who manufactures a world war for ratings in Tomorrow Never Dies
Emilio Largo — SPECTRE's Number 2 and Thunderball's antagonist; steals NATO nuclear warheads from an underwater base
Felix Leiter — Bond's perpetual CIA counterpart across multiple films and actors; the reliable American ally
Francisco Sanchez — Cocaine cartel kingpin in Licence to Kill; brutal, calculated, and genuinely dangerous
Franz Oberhauser — Blofeld's birth name; Spectre's revelation that Bond's greatest enemy was hiding in plain sight
Georgi Koskov — Scheming Soviet general who fakes a defection to manipulate both sides in The Living Daylights
Gobinda — Kamal Khan's silent, devastating Sikh bodyguard in Octopussy; fights on top of a moving plane
Gustav Graves — Diamond smuggler with a satellite weapon and a stolen identity; Die Another Day's main villain
Henry Gupta — Techno-terrorist in Elliot Carver's employ who provides the stolen decoder in Tomorrow Never Dies
Henry Stamper — Carver's brutal enforcer and the man who killed Wai Lin's partner in Tomorrow Never Dies
Hinx — SPECTRE's giant mute enforcer in Spectre (2015); killed mid-sentence by a case of champagne
Hugo Drax — Moonraker industrialist who plans to wipe out humanity and repopulate Earth with his master race
Jack Wade — Wisecracking CIA operative who assists Bond in GoldenEye and Tomorrow Never Dies
James Bond — MI6's 007; licensed to kill, perpetually underestimated, and never without a witty exit line
Janus — Alec Trevelyan's alias as the head of the GoldenEye crime syndicate; named for the two-faced god
Jaws — Richard Kiel's steel-toothed giant; the most beloved henchman of the Roger Moore era, appears in two films
Kamal Khan — Aristocratic Afghan smuggler and General Orlov's partner in the Octopussy jewel-smuggling scheme
Kananga — Mr. Big's real identity; Live and Let Die's voodoo-tied Harlem heroin lord with a compressed-gas death
Kerim Bey — Head of MI6's Istanbul station in From Russia with Love; charming, resourceful, and indispensable
Kincade — Skyfall estate gamekeeper; old retainer with a shotgun, a Land Rover, and the nerve to use both
Kronsteen — SPECTRE's chess-playing tactical mastermind in From Russia with Love; executed when the plan fails
Le Chiffre — SPECTRE banker and compulsive gambler; Casino Royale's cold-blooded card-table and torture-room villain
Leiter — (see Felix Leiter)
Locque — Eerily calm assassin who works for Kristatos in For Your Eyes Only; killed by Bond's kicked car
Lyutsifer Safin — Chemical weapons specialist and No Time to Die's antagonist; brooding island-dwelling mass murderer
M — Bernard Lee's original M; the no-nonsense spymaster who tolerates Bond more than he lets on
Mallory — Gareth Mallory; new M from Skyfall onward, former SAS and Northern Ireland veteran
Marc-Ange Draco — Corsican crime boss, father of Tracy, and Bond's surprisingly honorable ally in OHMSS
Mishkin — Russian Defense Minister briefly and uneasily allied with Bond during GoldenEye's endgame
Mollaka — Bomb-maker with extraordinary parkour skills; Bond pursues him in Casino Royale's pre-title chase
Morzeny — SPECTRE operative who leads the boat attack and the retrieval operation in From Russia with Love
Mr. Kidd — One half of the Wint-and-Kidd assassin duo in Diamonds Are Forever; dry humor, deadly efficiency
Mr. White — Quantum and SPECTRE's enigmatic liaison officer who threads through Casino Royale, QoS, and Spectre
Mr. Wint — The other half of the Wint-and-Kidd duo; the more overtly sinister of the pair
Nick Nack — Scaramanga's diminutive, scheming manservant in The Man with the Golden Gun; dangerous despite appearances
Oddjob — Goldfinger's silent Korean bodyguard; near-invulnerable physique, lethal razor-brimmed bowler hat
Orlov — General Orlov; rogue Soviet general in Octopussy who smuggles art treasures to fund a European invasion
Ourumov — General Arkady Ourumov; traitorous GoldenEye antagonist who assassinates 006 and allies with Janus
Pushkin — General Pushkin; Soviet KGB chief who plays dead and quietly allies with Bond in The Living Daylights
Q — Desmond Llewelyn's Q; MI6 Quartermaster who equipped Bond across nearly two decades of films with barely concealed exasperation
R — Q's successor briefly played by John Cleese in Die Another Day; inherits the exasperation intact
Raoul Silva — Skyfall's cyber-terrorist; former MI6 agent with a disfigured jaw and a deep, personal vendetta against M
Red Grant — SPECTRE's blond assassin and mirror-image foil to Bond in From Russia with Love; killed on the Orient Express
Renard — The World Is Not Enough's anarchist operative; a bullet in his brain eliminates his ability to feel pain
Scaramanga — Francisco Scaramanga; The Man with the Golden Gun, the world's most expensive hired assassin with a golden third nipple
Tanner — Bill Tanner; MI6 Chief of Staff and M's perennial aide, steady presence across multiple films
Tee Hee — Live and Let Die's hook-handed henchman; politely menacing enforcer for Kananga's operation
Tiger Tanaka — Head of Japan's secret service JIS; suave, resourceful, and one of Bond's most capable foreign allies
Valentin Zukovsky — Russian ex-KGB arms dealer with an old grudge and an ambiguous alliance with Bond across two films
Whitaker — (see Brad Whitaker)
Zao — Die Another Day henchman with diamonds embedded in his face from a failed mission; Chang's replacement
Zorin — Max Zorin; A View to a Kill's psychopathic microchip industrialist with KGB origins and no moral floor

### Metal Gear

Big Boss — The legendary soldier, real name John; his genes and legacy shaped the entire Metal Gear saga
Chico — Young Sandinista soldier taken prisoner and held at Camp Omega; appears in Ground Zeroes
Code Talker — Aged Navajo biochemist who developed the vocal cord parasites and serves as an unlikely ally in The Phantom Pain
Coldman — Hardline CIA officer who is willing to launch a nuclear strike just to prove deterrence theory works in Peace Walker
Decoy Octopus — FOXHOUND's master impersonator; spends MGS1 disguised as DARPA chief Donald Anderson until it kills him
Drebin 893 — Arms launderer and reluctant Snake ally who operates from a tank, provides ID-locked weapon removal in MGS4
Frank Jaeger — Gray Fox; cyborg ninja, Bond's most eloquent line delivery, and the series' most tragic figure
Huey Emmerich — Otacon's father; a wheelchair-bound Metal Gear designer who betrays everyone around him repeatedly
Kazuhira Miller — Kaz; Diamond Dogs XO and MSF commander with a fierce anti-Snake loyalty and a missing arm
Liquid Ocelot — Ocelot with a psychosomatically implanted Liquid persona; final boss of MGS4 and the series' last hurrah
Liquid Snake — Eli; Solid Snake's twin and the saga's most charismatic antagonist; convinced he got the inferior genes
Master Miller — Delta Force trainer and Snake's mentor; appears throughout MGS1's codec and is already dead by the time you hear him
Otacon — Hal Emmerich; pacifist otaku turned Philanthropy co-founder who cries at everything and hacks everything
Psycho Mantis — Telepathic FOXHOUND operative who reads your memory card, moves your controller, and breaks the fourth wall
Revolver Ocelot — Adamska; the saga's consummate triple-crosser whose loyalties run deeper than anyone suspected
Skull Face — XOF commander disfigured by nuclear fire; fanatical anti-American language terrorist with a nuclear arsenal
Solidus Snake — George Sears; former US President, third Big Boss clone, and the Sons of the Patriots' secret architect
Solid Snake — David; the man sent into Outer Heaven to destroy Metal Gear, and into every subsequent crisis after that
Sokolov — Nikolai Sokolov; Soviet rocket scientist whose rescue is the mission that begins the entire Big Boss origin saga
Vamp — Romanian-born Dead Cell member with unexplained regeneration; survives everything until nanomachines son
Venom Snake — The Phantom; the medic who became Big Boss's body double and built Diamond Dogs in The Phantom Pain
Volgin — Colonel Yevgeny Volgin; sadistic GRU commander who runs electricity through enemies for pleasure in Snake Eater
Vulcan Raven — Massive FOXHOUND shaman warrior who commands a cloud of ravens and guards the communications tower
Zero — Major Zero; founder of the Patriots and Big Boss's commander-turned-ideological adversary across the whole timeline

### Mortal Kombat

Baraka — Tarkatan warrior with retractable arm blades; fiercely loyal to Shao Kahn and ruthless in kombat
Bo' Rai Cho — Drunken master and Liu Kang's trainer; deceptively dangerous beneath the belching and stumbling
Cyrax — Yellow-armored Cyber Lin Kuei warrior who spends most of the series struggling to reclaim his humanity
Daegon — Forgotten god who awakened prematurely and founded the Red Dragon clan to pursue his agenda
Ermac — Telekinetic warrior formed from the fused souls of Outworld's fallen; more minds than one in a single body
Fujin — Wind god and Raiden's fellow Elder guardian of Earthrealm; more measured than his thunder counterpart
Geras — Immortal servant of Kronika who resets each time he is killed; effectively unkillable in a straight fight
Goro — Shokan prince and four-armed Grand Champion of Mortal Kombat; holds the title for 500 years before Cage
Havik — Clergyman of Chaos from Orderrealm's opposite; fights to spread disorder across all realms
Hsu Hao — Red Dragon operative planted inside the Outer World Investigation Agency as a long-term mole
Jax Briggs — U.S. Special Forces major with cybernetic arms; Sonya's partner, later commanding officer
Johnny Cage — Hollywood action star and founding Earthrealm defender; earns genuine respect across multiple timelines
Kabal — Former Black Dragon enforcer disfigured by Shao Kahn's forces; rebuilt with a respirator and unmatched speed
Kai — Earthrealm monk who trained under Bo' Rai Cho alongside Liu Kang before heading on a solo spiritual journey
Kano — Black Dragon mercenary with a cybernetic eye; brutal, opportunistic, and reliably treacherous to Earthrealm
Kenshi — Blind swordsman with telekinetic powers; seeks revenge against Shang Tsung for stealing his sight and his heritage
Kollector — Naknadan tax collector who served Shao Kahn across lifetimes; hoards the possessions of the fallen dead
Kotal Kahn — Outworld emperor with Aztec-inspired origins; wields blood magic and solar power, rules through overwhelming force
Kung Jin — Shaolin archer and Kung Lao's cousin; one of the younger Earthrealm defenders with a troubled past
Kung Lao — Shaolin monk and Great Kung Lao's descendant; razor-hat wielder who fights in Liu Kang's shadow and resents it
Liu Kang — Shaolin champion and Earthrealm's chosen defender; wins the first tournament and bears the weight of every timeline
Motaro — Centaurian general of Shao Kahn's armies; partially immune to projectile attacks, an Outworld enforcer
Nightwolf — Matoka warrior and shaman who serves as Earthrealm's spiritual anchor and one of its steadiest defenders
Noob Saibot — The original Sub-Zero resurrected as a shadow wraith; serves the Brotherhood of Shadow and his own dark agenda
Onaga — The Dragon King; Outworld's original ruler whose undead army threatens every realm simultaneously
Quan Chi — Netherrealm sorcerer who collects souls, plays every side against itself, and ultimately serves Shinnok
Rain — Edenian demi-god with water and lightning powers; chronically betrays every alliance he enters for personal gain
Raiden — Thunder god and Earthrealm's immortal protector; makes increasingly desperate sacrifices to prevent extinction
Reiko — Shao Kahn's ambitious general who secretly covets the emperor's throne; wields a shuriken mace
Reptile — Reptilian ninja from the near-extinct Saurian race; loyal to anyone who promises to restore his people
Scorpion — Hanzo Hasashi; undead Netherrealm ninja specter driven by vengeance for his clan and his family
Sektor — Red-armored Cyber Lin Kuei grandmaster; unlike Cyrax, has no ambivalence about losing his humanity
Shao Kahn — Conqueror Emperor of Outworld; the series' most iconic recurring villain and an unmovable wall of brutality
Shinnok — Fallen Elder God imprisoned in the Netherrealm; commands bone-rending dark magic and endless spite
Shujinko — Wandering kombatant tricked by Onaga into gathering the Kamidogu across realms over an entire lifetime
Smoke — Ninja revenant and Sub-Zero's closest ally; turned into a cyber-unit, later freed, always loyal
Stryker — NYPD riot control officer; an ordinary cop thrown into Outworld's extraordinary scale of warfare
Sub-Zero — Kuai Liang; the second Sub-Zero who masters ice manipulation and eventually leads and reforms Lin Kuei
Takashi Takeda — Special Forces recruit and Kenshi's son; a telepathic whip-armed fighter trained by Scorpion
Taven — Forgotten god and protagonist of Armageddon; intended to prevent the apocalypse, manipulated throughout

### Predator

Billy Sole — The team's tracker; chooses to face the Predator alone with a machete rather than run
Blain Cooper — "I ain't got time to bleed"; cigar-chomping mercenary with a minigun who dies before anyone believes the threat
Dillon — CIA operative embedded in Dutch's team under false pretenses; the mission's real author and its first casualty among the command
Dutch — Alan "Dutch" Schaefer; Predator's muscle-bound commandant and the only member of his team to survive
Mac Eliot — Blain's closest comrade; consumed entirely by grief-rage after Blain's death, hunts the Predator into the trees
Mike Harrigan — LAPD detective who confronts and kills the Predator in Los Angeles using its own weapon in Predator 2
Poncho — Demo expert in Dutch's team; wounded early and carried out before the final confrontation

### Quake

Anarki — Anarchist punk with a self-administered syringe and a skateboard; one of Quake Champions' most chaotic fighters
Bitterman — Marine protagonist of Quake II; invades the Strogg homeworld solo and fights his way to the Makron
Clutch — Robotic Quake Champions fighter; industrial-strength exoskeleton with passive defense and heavy damage
Doom — Arena fighter in Quake III Arena; the fully armored combatant whose name is a direct nod to id Software's legacy
Enforcer — Elite laser-armed soldier guarding Quake's base levels; human in armor but mechanical in discipline
Keel — Stroggified marine who fought through Quake II's campaign and retained enough of himself to keep going
Klesk — Insectoid alien arena fighter from Quake III and Champions; rapid, chitinous, and relentlessly aggressive
Makron — Strogg supreme commander and Quake II's final boss; the emperor of alien cyborg conquest
Matthew Kane — Marine protagonist of Quake 4; forced to undergo partial Stroggification mid-campaign and fights through it
Orbb — Spherical surveillance drone repurposed as an arena combatant in Quake III; entirely inhuman aesthetic
Phobos — Space marine arena fighter in Quake III; the quintessential armored soldier combatant
Ranger — The original Quake protagonist; a soldier lost in dimension-hopping time portals, fighting Lovecraftian horrors
Sarge — Quake III's heavily armored military veteran; no-nonsense, brutal at close range, covered in paint and scars
Scalebearer — Quake Champions heavy; a warlord from a subjugated alien world turned into an unstoppable arena weapon
Sorlag — Acid-spitting Sorg alien fighter in Quake III and Champions; driven primarily by carnivorous instinct
Tank Jr. — Quake III arena fighter styled after the Quake II Tank enemy; smaller than the original, equally lethal
Uriel — Armored Quake III fighter with an angular, almost ecclesiastical battle-monk aesthetic
Visor — Tactical Quake III combatant whose visor provides unparalleled awareness of the entire arena at all times
Wrack — Quake Champions fighter with scavenged armor over a near-skeletal frame; efficient and lightweight
Xaero — Quake III's final arena boss; the undefeated champion before Ranger, and the most dangerous fighter in the game

### Rainbow Six

Ding Chavez — Domingo Chavez; Rainbow's perennial field operative who eventually rises to command the unit
Eddie Price — British SAS operator and one of Rainbow's founding senior members; steady, experienced, lethal
Homer Johnston — American FBI hostage-rescue expert and Rainbow's primary sharpshooter
John Clark — John Kelly; CIA officer and former Navy SEAL who builds Rainbow from the ground up
Paul Bellow — Team psychologist in the Rainbow Six novel who evaluates operator fitness for field deployment
Tim Noonan — FBI technical expert specializing in electronic surveillance and signals intelligence with Rainbow

### Resident Evil

Albert Wesker — STARS captain turned bioweapons orchestrator; pursues godhood across multiple continents and games
Barry Burton — STARS weapons expert and loyal comrade; spends most of RE1 working under Umbrella's blackmail
Billy Coen — Disgraced Marine convicted of war crimes; protagonist of RE0 whose guilt runs deeper than his record
Brad Vickers — STARS helicopter pilot nicknamed Chickenheart; abandons his team in RE1 and pays for it in RE3
Carlos Oliveira — Friendly Umbrella mercenary who defects during RE3; warmth and humor contrast with Nicholai's coldness
Chris Redfield — STARS Alpha team's muscle who eventually punches boulders and dismantles Wesker's global network
Ethan Winters — Ordinary man thrown into RE7 and RE8's grotesque horrors; stubborn, resourceful, and deeply paternal
HUNK — Umbrella's most durable extraction operative; the only one who ever walks out; never identified beyond the codename
Jack Krauser — U.S. military agent transformed by Las Plagas; Leon's former partner and one of Saddler's enforcers in RE4
Jake Muller — Wesker's son and carrier of the C-Virus antibody; RE6 co-protagonist whose immunity makes him a target
Leon Kennedy — Rookie cop turned government agent; the most charismatic protagonist in the series and the most quotable
Luis Sera — Spanish researcher who secretly helps Leon in RE4 despite working for Saddler; charming, doomed
Nicholai Ginovaef — Umbrella Watchdog mercenary who sells B.O.W. performance data to the highest bidder in RE3
Piers Nivans — BSAA operator and Chris's loyal partner in RE6; injects himself with a mutagenic agent to save Chris
Richard Aiken — STARS Bravo team's radio operator; kills himself protecting Rebecca from the Yawn in the Spencer Mansion

### Street Fighter

Abel — French amnesiac grappler with combat training of unknown origin; connected to Shadaloo's supersoldier experiments
Adon — Muay Thai prodigy and Sagat's former student; abandons his mentor to chase Akuma's power instead
Akuma — Gouki; walked the path of the Satsui no Hado to its murderous extreme and killed his own brother Gouken
Alex — Street Fighter III's American grappler protagonist; enters the tournament to avenge his mentor Tom's brutal beating
Balrog — M. Bison's American enforcer; a banned heavyweight boxer who works for Shadaloo purely for money and violence
Birdie — Imposing British punk who defected from Shadaloo after learning just how expendable Bison considers his underlings
Blanka — Jimmy; Brazilian feral fighter mutated by electricity during a plane crash; fierce loyalty to his mother
Cody Travers — Former street-fighting hero of Metro City who spent years in prison and stopped caring about heroism
Dan Hibiki — Founder of Saikyo-style fighting; trains with limitless enthusiasm despite objectively limited talent
Dee Jay — Jamaican kickboxer and musician; the most relentlessly cheerful combatant in the World Warrior tournament
Dhalsim — Indian yogi who fights to fund his village; can stretch his limbs, breathe fire, and teleport at will
E. Honda — Sumo grand champion on a personal mission to prove sumo's worth to every fighting style in the world
Fei Long — Hong Kong action star directly inspired by Bruce Lee; enters the tournament to protect martial arts from exploitation
Gill — Street Fighter III's Illuminati emperor; half-fire, half-ice body, and a deeply messianic self-regard
Gouken — Ryu and Ken's master; survived Akuma's supposedly lethal strike through the Power of Nothingness and returned in SF4
Guile — U.S. Air Force officer who entered the Street Fighter tournament to avenge his dead friend Charlie Nash
Guy — Metro City ninja and Haggar's ally in Final Fight; practices the Bushinryu ninjutsu style passed down through lineage
Ken Masters — American martial artist and Ryu's closest rival; fights with passion and flair where Ryu fights with discipline
M. Bison — Dictator; Shadaloo's supreme leader who discards and resurrects his own body using Psycho Power at will
Nash — Charlie Nash; Guile's fallen friend resurrected as a grey-skinned revenant with a missing chunk of his skull in SF5
Rolento — Former Mad Gear lieutenant turned mercenary; obsessed with building a perfect military nation from the ruins
Ryu — The wandering warrior who pursues the true essence of fighting with no attachment to victory, rank, or reward
Sagat — Emperor of Muay Thai who bears Ryu's Shoryuken scar across his chest; finds something resembling redemption
Sean — Ken's Brazilian student; earnest and outmatched, but hero-worships Ken with an enthusiasm that carries him far
Seth — Street Fighter IV's final boss; a Shadaloo weapon system housing a copy of every fighter's techniques
Sodom — Mad Gear enforcer with a samurai obsession; mangles every Japanese phrase he attempts but keeps trying
T. Hawk — Native American warrior fighting to reclaim his tribe's homeland from Bison's Shadaloo occupation
Urien — Gill's power-hungry younger brother; fights nearly naked and considers this entirely appropriate
Vega — Bison's Spanish narcissist assassin; fights with a claw and a cage, considers ugliness a personal offense
Yang — One of the SF3 twin fighters from Hong Kong; more tactical and less boastful than his brother Yun
Yun — One of the SF3 twins; cocky Hong Kong street fighter who arrives on a skateboard and talks nonstop
Zangief — Soviet wrestling icon dispatched by Gorbachev to represent Russian strength in the World Warrior tournament

### Tekken

Armor King — Mysterious masked wrestler with a personal agenda; mentor and dark mirror to King
Bruce Irvin — Muay Thai champion recruited by Kazuya as his personal bodyguard and combat enforcer
Bryan Fury — Resurrected detective turned Mishima cyborg; fights entirely for the joy of violence with no other motive
Craig Marduk — Australian vale tudo champion whose feud with King over the original Armor King defines his arc
Eddy Gordo — Brazilian capoeira fighter seeking justice for his father's murder and the Mishima machinery behind it
Feng Wei — Chinese martial artist obsessed with obtaining the God Fist scroll and crushing every other style
Forest Law — Marshall Law's son who enters the King of Iron Fist Tournament behind his father's back
Gun Jack — Upgraded Jack-series combat robot rebuilt by Jane; protective of his creator and devastating in the field
Heihachi Mishima — Tekken's iron-willed patriarch who threw his own son off a cliff and trained his grandson to kill
Hwoarang — Korean Taekwondo prodigy and Jin's primary rival; driven by the fight itself more than any cause
Jin Kazama — Heir to the Devil Gene; fights to end the Mishima bloodline's curse even if it means ending himself
Jinpachi Mishima — Tekken 5's final boss; Heihachi's father revived and possessed by an evil entity seeking release
Kazuya Mishima — Fell off a cliff as a boy, made a deal with the Devil to survive, and threw Heihachi back over one
King — Mysterious masked wrestler who fights to fund his orphanage; continues the legacy of Armor King
Lars Alexandersson — Tekken 6's protagonist; Heihachi's illegitimate son who leads a military rebellion against his own family
Lee Chaolan — Heihachi's adopted Chinese son; fights with elegant precision to spite Kazuya and remain relevant
Lei Wulong — Hong Kong supercop and master of drunken five-animal kung fu styles; investigates the Mishima zaibatsu
Marshall Law — Jeet Kune Do instructor who enters every tournament to pay his son's mounting hospital bills
Paul Phoenix — American martial artist who genuinely believes he is the strongest in the universe; loses to a bear
Raven — Ninja operative from an unnamed intelligence agency; fights with iaijutsu speed and an eerie calm
Roger — Combat kangaroo from a military experiment; enters Tekken 2 with functional if unusual boxing
Sergei Dragunov — Silent Spetsnaz operative fighting in combat sambo; completely expressionless, lethally effective
Steve Fox — British boxer who enters the tournament and discovers a disturbing connection to the Mishima conspiracy
Yoshimitsu — Manji Clan leader and space-sword wielder; ally to the poor, thorn in the zaibatsu's side

### Terminator

Cromartie — T-888 infiltration unit in The Sarah Connor Chronicles; exceptionally focused and methodical in pursuit
John Connor — Humanity's future commander against Skynet; his existence is the entire hinge of the time war
Kyle Reese — Sent back from 2029 to protect Sarah Connor; becomes John's father and seals the loop
Marcus Wright — Death-row inmate whose body Skynet harvested to build a human-machine hybrid in Salvation
Miles Dyson — Cyberdyne's AI researcher whose work on the CPU creates Skynet; dies destroying it in T2
Skynet — The self-aware defense network that launched nuclear war on Judgment Day to ensure its own survival
T-800 — Cyberdyne Systems Model 101; the original infiltration endoskeleton in Arnold Schwarzenegger's form
T-1000 — Advanced mimetic poly-alloy liquid-metal prototype; far beyond the T-800 in adaptability and menace

### The Matrix

Agent Brown — One of the three Agents initially pursuing Neo; a program designed to police the simulation's dissidents
Agent Jones — The third Agent alongside Smith and Brown; executes the suppression of redpills with mechanical indifference
Agent Smith — Matrix enforcer who evolves beyond his programming into a self-replicating existential virus
Apoc — Morpheus's crew member and the Nebuchadnezzar's driver; one of the first to die after Cypher's betrayal
Architect — Creator of the Matrix who reveals to Neo that the prophecy itself is a control mechanism
Bane — Zion crew member from another ship whose body is hijacked by Agent Smith in Reloaded and Revolutions
Cypher — Reagan; Morpheus's bitter crew member who trades his teammates' lives for steak and blissful re-insertion
Dozer — The Nebuchadnezzar's powerplant operator; born free outside the Matrix and never jacked in
Ghost — Niobe's copilot and the most spiritually grounded of Zion's fighters; carries twin pistols and quiet conviction
Link — The Nebuchadnezzar's operator in Reloaded and Revolutions; replaces Tank and keeps the crew connected
Morpheus — Captain of the Nebuchadnezzar and the true believer who spent his life searching for the One
Mouse — The youngest crew member; the Nebuchadnezzar's digital pimp who dies holding the phone line open
Neo — Thomas A. Anderson; the One who rewrites the rules of the Matrix and negotiates peace with the machines
Seraph — Oracle's golden guardian and former exile who chose protection over power; defeated Agent Smith and walked away
Tank — The Nebuchadnezzar's operator in the first film; born free, never jacked in, the crew's true anchor

### Tombstone

Billy Clanton — Hot-headed young member of the Cowboys; killed in the O.K. Corral gunfight
Curly Bill Brocius — Charismatic and dangerous leader of the Cowboys; shot by Wyatt Earp at Iron Springs
Doc Holliday — Consumptive gambler and the deadliest gunslinger in the film; Wyatt's most loyal and lethal ally
Frank McLaury — Cowboy member and capable fighter killed in the O.K. Corral confrontation
Ike Clanton — Cowardly Cowboy whose drunken boasting directly triggers the O.K. Corral fight
Johnny Ringo — The Cowboys' most dangerous gunman; haunted, intellectual, and Doc Holliday's ultimate rival
Morgan Earp — Youngest of the lawman Earps; assassinated during a billiards game, triggering Wyatt's vendetta ride
Sherman McMasters — Recruited by Wyatt for the vendetta posse in the aftermath of Morgan's murder
Texas Jack Vermillion — Frontiersman and marksman who joins Wyatt's vendetta ride against the Cowboys
Tom McLaury — Cowboy killed at the O.K. Corral; his death alongside his brother inflamed the conflict that followed
Turkey Creek Jack Johnson — Veteran scout and another member of Wyatt Earp's vendetta posse
Virgil Earp — Tombstone's marshal and eldest Earp brother; crippled in an assassination attempt after the Corral
Wyatt Earp — Town marshal, faro dealer, and Tombstone's enduring symbol of frontier law and cold resolve

### Twisted Metal

Axel — Bound between two giant wheels by his cruel father; drives the tournament seeking the strength to break free
Black — A darker alternate Calypso figure in Twisted Metal: Black; the tournament's soul twisted inward
Calypso — The tournament's mysterious organizer who grants wishes in the most cruelly literal way imaginable
Grimm — Charlie Kane; death-obsessed biker haunted by a Vietnam-era atrocity he caused and cannot escape
Minion — Massive supernatural vehicle that serves as Calypso's enforcer and muscle across multiple entries
Needles Kane — Sweet Tooth; clown-masked serial killer in an ice cream truck with a flaming head; the series' icon
No-Face — Disfigured man who enters Twisted Metal seeking to get his face back; tragic, grotesque, and desperate
Preacher — Conflicted man of faith who wrestles with his capacity for violence and what divine purpose means

### Unforgiven

Beauchamp — W.W. Beauchamp; dime novelist who follows English Bob and then Little Bill, mythologizing whatever he sees
English Bob — The Duke of Death; pompous British hired gun publicly dismantled by Little Bill's brutal lesson in reality
Little Bill Daggett — Big Whiskey's sadistic sheriff who genuinely believes in his own version of order, however violent
Ned Logan — William's oldest friend and partner; more decent than the life they chose, and he pays for that decency
Schofield Kid — Young would-be gunfighter who discovers in the moment of truth that he cannot live with what killing means
William Munny — Retired outlaw and widowed father of two; drawn back into killing and reminded of exactly who he is

### Unreal Tournament

Arkon — Iron Guard warrior with a tactically aggressive arena playstyle
Brock — Male Juggernaut team fighter in Unreal Tournament 2004; heavy and imposing
Gorge — Iron Guard's captain; heavy-hitting arena combatant and squad anchor
Malcolm — The undefeated Thundercrash champion before UT's events; the face of the franchise and its defining player
Matrix — Elite Juggernauts fighter with a calculated, mechanical approach to arena combat
Othello — Iron Guard member and reliable all-around arena team fighter
Reaper — Ronin team leader in Unreal Tournament 3; a warrior carrying personal scores into every match
Riker — Mid-tier arena combatant known for consistent mechanical performance across maps
Viktor — Iron Guard fighter in Unreal Tournament 3; part of the heavy-hitting squad composition
Xan Kriegor — The Corrupt's robotic champion; first True champion of the tournament, near-invincible mechanical arena god

### Wolfenstein

B.J. Blazkowicz — William Joseph Blazkowicz; the Allied supersoldier tearing through Nazi fortresses across every timeline
Bombate — Kreisau Circle resistance fighter and one of B.J.'s most capable comrades in New Order and New Colossus
Deathshead — General Strasse; the Third Reich's chief scientist obsessed with augmented soldier programs and immortality
Fergus Reid — B.J.'s Scottish comrade and Kreisau Circle veteran; one of two soldiers whose fate B.J. must choose
Max Hass — Gentle giant of the Kreisau Circle; brain-damaged, childlike in affect, but fiercely loyal and capable
Set Roth — Kreisau Circle's elderly Jewish professor; his inventions and knowledge are decisive in defeating Deathshead
Super Spesh — Conspiracy theorist and Kreisau fighter with unhinged energy; wrong about almost everything, useful regardless
Wyatt — Young American Kreisau member; the other soldier whose survival depends on B.J.'s choice in the timeline split
